items.title: Underfitting vs overfitting
items.description: Augmentation mitigates variance-limited overfitting (low train, high val). It does not fix underfitting when both errors stay high — capacity or features must change first.

items.title: Supervised learning
items.description: Augmentation assumes labeled pairs (X, y) where transforms preserve y — the core supervised setup. Unsupervised augmentation (contrastive views) is a different paradigm.

items.title: Early stopping
items.description: Early stopping halts training when validation error worsens; augmentation reduces how quickly the model memorizes training rows. Teams often use both on small image and text sets.

items.title: Cleaning and transformation
items.description: Pipeline cleaning fixes labels and schema; augmentation runs after valid rows exist and applies training-time diversity transforms — distinct from imputation or normalization logic.

items.title: Generalization
items.description: Well-chosen augmentation teaches invariances that match deployment inputs, narrowing the train–validation gap when overfitting drove poor generalization.
